Toastmasters Chapter

Leaders of Tomorrow

Find Your Voice. Share Your Story. Lead the Way.

Join a Meeting Learn More

About Our Chapter

Leaders of Tomorrow is a Toastmasters chapter dedicated to empowering individuals to become more confident speakers, effective communicators, and better leaders through regular, constructive feedback and practice.

All money raised through the chapter goes directly to Artists of Tomorrow, an international project that promotes the arts and creative expression around the world. When you grow as a speaker with us, you are also helping young artists everywhere find their voice.

A Safe Place to Grow

Everyone starts out feeling nervous — and that is perfectly okay. We provide a safe and supportive place to grow where mistakes are embraced as part of the learning process. The goal is steady progress, not perfection.

No matter what role a member holds, they are expected to be respectful, quiet while others are speaking, encouraging, and kind. When you walk into a Leaders of Tomorrow meeting, you walk into a community that wants to see you succeed.

How Our Meetings Work

Every session is carefully structured so members can practise, receive feedback, and improve — all within a welcoming, collaborative atmosphere.

1

Opening

Our President opens the meeting, welcomes members and guests, and sets the tone for the session.

2

Prepared Speeches

Members deliver speeches they have researched and rehearsed, practising structure, storytelling, and delivery.

3

Feedback Sessions

Constructive, encouraging evaluations help every speaker understand their strengths and discover areas to refine.

4

Table Topics

Impromptu speaking rounds challenge members to think on their feet and respond confidently to surprise questions.

Club Roles

Our meetings are highly organised and collaborative. Members rotate through a range of roles that develop different leadership and communication skills.

Toastmaster

Runs the meeting and introduces each speaker, keeping the energy high and the agenda on track.

Ah Counter

Helps speakers become more polished by counting filler words like "um," "uh," and "like."

Timer

Keeps every segment running smoothly, signalling speakers so they learn to manage their time effectively.

General Evaluator

Provides overarching feedback on the meeting as a whole, highlighting what went well and what can improve.

Table Topics Master

Selects impromptu questions and leads the Table Topics session, keeping speakers engaged and on their toes.

Speech Categories

Members gain experience through a rich variety of speaking formats, each building a different dimension of communication mastery.

Speeches to Inform

Teach and educate your audience using clear facts, compelling examples, and well-organised ideas.

Persuasive Speeches

Guide others toward a decision using honesty, logic, and mutual benefits — the art of ethical influence.

Entertaining Speeches

Captivate audiences through vivid stories, humour, and witty jokes that leave a lasting impression.

Core Skills You'll Build

Whether you are a complete beginner or an experienced speaker looking to sharpen your edge, our chapter focuses on the fundamentals that make great communicators:

  • A Strong, Clear Voice — Project with confidence so every word lands with impact.
  • Confident Posture — Stand tall and grounded to command the room before you say a word.
  • Meaningful Eye Contact — Connect with your audience on a human level and hold their attention.
  • Calm Under Pressure — Learn techniques to appear confident even when you feel anxious inside.

Join Us at Bell Works Library

Leaders of Tomorrow meets at the Bell Works Library — a vibrant, inspiring space that is the perfect backdrop for finding your voice. Whether you want to conquer a fear of public speaking, grow as a leader, or simply meet a community of supportive people, you belong here.

Every meeting you attend helps fund Artists of Tomorrow and the young artists we champion around the world. Your growth as a speaker directly powers creative expression for children everywhere.

Get in Touch Support Our Mission
